title: Adiantopsis fordii
taxonomy: Adiantopsis fordii is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Adiantopsis and is native to South America. The species is further classified as Adiantopsis fordii (Mett.) Fée, indicating its classification by French botanist Élie-Abel Carrière's student, Élie-Abel Carrière's student's, French botanist Fernand Fée in 1869. The classification places it within the Pteridophyta division of plants. Adiantopsis fordii is a terrestrial fern, which typically thrives in subtropical and tropical regions.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds.
